frameworks.name: reinforcement learning pretraining
frameworks.one_line: RL from scratch as pretraining objective to learn reasoning.

frameworks.name: next-token prediction pretraining
frameworks.one_line: Standard LLM pretraining argued to entangle reasoning with knowledge.

frameworks.name: curriculum learning
frameworks.one_line: Synthetic-task curriculum bootstraps a transferable reasoning prior.

frameworks.name: artificial general intelligence
frameworks.one_line: Target capability contrasted with artificial useful intelligence (AUI).

core_question: How can large models acquire transferable reasoning rather than knowledge-coupled pattern matching?
key_assumptions: Reasoning and knowledge are separable capacities
RL objectives can shape reasoning priors absent linguistic supervision
Small context windows reduce spurious token correlations
manipulations: Algorithmic tasks expressed in esoteric programming languages
Pretraining objective: RL-from-scratch vs next-token prediction
key_findings: LLMs succeed on familiar reasoning but fail to transfer algorithmic understanding to novel esoteric languages
Observed reasoning overfits training-data surface form rather than generalizing
interpretations: Coupling of reasoning and knowledge in LLMs underlies fragile transfer
Disentangling via RL pretraining, synthetic curricula, and small contexts is a path from AUI to AGI
